solute.description: This molecule features a fused bicyclic 4-oxo-quinoline core substituted with an $N$-ethyl group at the 1-position and a carboxylic acid group at the 3-position. The aromatic portion of the core is further functionalized with a fluorine atom at the 6-position and a 4-methylpiperazine ring at the 7-position. It is a moderately sized, highly polar molecule characterized by significant hydrogen bonding capacity, acting as both a donor via the carboxylic acid and an acceptor through the carbonyl, fluorine, and multiple nitrogen centers.

solvent.description: This molecule is a linear, unbranched aliphatic chain consisting of eight carbon atoms. It features a single primary hydroxyl group (-OH) covalently bonded to the terminal carbon atom, characterizing it as a medium-chain primary alcohol. The structure is predominantly nonpolar due to the long hydrocarbon tail, but it possesses a localized polar head capable of both donating and accepting hydrogen bonds. Its distinctive feature is the specific balance between the bulky eight-carbon hydrophobic chain and the single hydrophilic functional group.
